mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are statistically higher when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power when UNRATE increases by less than 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over 60-day horizons, or returns are negative when both series trend upward concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects weakening manufacturing output that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions, or Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero across all expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.5% forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, which compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the threshold shows no statistical separation from baseline return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate profit margins and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 is negative or zero over 5-day horizons, or 5-day forward returns decline when quarterly GDP growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive following >1.0% monthly INDPRO declines during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive significance outside contraction periods.
